page: Microsoft Azure DS12 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the DS server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core and includes 28.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.0 GB per core. It also includes 57 GB of local storage, with no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between compute and memory capabilities. The CPU performance is generally positioned in the lower tier, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core results, Geekbench scores, and Passmark CPU scores falling into the bottom 25 percent. However, the memory subsystem demonstrates moderate capability, with memory bandwidth and Passmark memory scores ranking in the middle 50 percent. Redis SET operations perform at an average level of 6.15 million operations per second, and small-model LLM inference achieves average speeds of 415.0 tokens per second for prompt processing and 100.2 tokens per second for text generation.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the DS12 offers a high memory allocation relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for workloads that require substantial memory capacity but do not demand intensive CPU processing. The presence of 57 GB of local storage adds utility for temporary data caching. This resource profile is best suited for memory-intensive general-purpose workloads, caching layers, lightweight database operations, and moderate Redis deployments where memory capacity is prioritized over raw compute throughput.

This instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy, compute-light workloads.
